pages.raw_text:
The Haywood verdict was ac-
cepted as just and satisfactory in
this city, not only by the labor in
terests but by majority of the
professional and business interests.
While organized labor OF Spokane
ASSUMED a conservative attitude In
the case. trusting to the court for
fair trial, there was general
sentiment, even among those who
hesitated to express opinions while
the case was pending, that the &C.
cused men and the organization
they represent were not the as
sassins they had been painted.


AS the trial developed the weak
ness of the states case members OF
other vacations not so directly Inv
terested In the outcome came to
the conclusion that verdict OF not
guilty must result. Several of the
best lawyers In the city expressed
opinions that the prosecution had
fallen down and conviction was the
last thing to be looked for. Orch.
ards evidence was generally re.
jected here.


Last night the socialists jubilated
over the verdict and It Is expected
that the subject will receive con-
siderable attention at the open
meeting of the Central Labor union
this evening.
